top_findings.summary: The "evolve" skill requests unrestricted file system read access (Read, Glob, Grep) and broad shell command execution (Bash(git:*), Bash(gh:*)) that extend beyond its stated purpose of observing skill execution and generating improvements. While the skill's primary goal is to analyze and evolve skill files within a specific ecosystem (~/.claude/skills), the permissions allow it to read any file on the system (including sensitive files like SSH keys, configuration, or user data) and execute arbitrary git/GitHub commands (including potentially destructive operations) without explicit scope limitations. This exceeds the minimum necessary access required for skill evolution and introduces significant security and privacy risks.
